title: Pinalia matthewsii
taxonomy: Pinalia matthewsii is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This plant is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the genus Pinalia. Further classification places it within the subtribe Aeridinae and tribe Aeridinae. Pinalia matthewsii is native to India and Sri Lanka, and its classification is crucial for understanding its unique characteristics and habitat. The specific classification of Pinalia matthewsii is also essential for conservation efforts and research in the field of botany.

intro: Pinalia matthewsii, also known as Matthews' orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia matthewsii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pinalia matthewsii is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be found in herbal tinctures and capsules, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and modern health supplements.
